DISCOVERY 610, DEFENDANT WALGREEN CO.'S MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS, OR IN THE ALTERNATIVE, ISSUE SANCTIONS AND REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS. 1. MOTION FOR TERMINATING SANCTIONS DENIED; 2. MOTION FOR ISSUE SANCTIONS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF SHALL BE PRECLUDED AT TRIAL FROM OFFERING OR ENTERING ANY EVIDENCE OF MEDICAL COSTS, EXPENSES OR DAMAGES; OR WAGE LOSS; AND SHALL BE PRECLUDED FROM OFFERING ANY EVIDENCE IN SUPPORT OF ALLEGATIONS OF NEBLIGENCE, PREMISES LIABILITY, OR FALSE IMPRISONMENT WITH REGARD OT HER CLAIM. 3. REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS GRANTED; MONETARY SANCTIONS SHALL ISSUE IN THE SUM OF $560.00. COMMISSIONER: PAUL SLAVIT. NOT REPORTED.

MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Mar-23-2009 Off Calendar

Notice And Motion For Terminating Sanctions Or In The Alternative, Issue Sanctions; And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Mar-18-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Dennis P. Fitzsimons, Esq., for defendant and moving party: Walgreen Co.

No other appearance, tentative ruling is adopted.
Defendant Walgreen Co.'s Motion For Terminating Sanctions, Or In The Alternative, Issue Sanctions And Request For Monetary Sanctions.
1. Motion for terminating sanctions denied;
2. Motion for issue Sanctions granted.
Plaintiff shall be precluded at trial from offering or entering any vidence of medical costs, expenses or damages; or wage loss; and shall be precluded from offering any vidence in support of allegations of nebligence, premises liability, or false imprisonment with regard ot her claim.
3. Request for monetary sanctions granted; monetary sanctions shall issue in the sum of $560.00. Commissioner: Paul Slavit. Not reported.

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Mar-13-2009 Continued To Apr-01-09 At 02:30 P.M. In Dept 505

Notice And Motion For Terminating Sanctions Or In The Alternative, Issue Sanctions; And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Feb-11-2009 Continued To May-18-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 610
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance, tentative ruling is adopted. On the Court's Defendant's motion for terminating sanctions is continued to May 18, 2009 at 9:00 a.m. as plaintiff remains incarcerated. The Court shall notify the parties of the continued hearing date. Judge: Bruce Chan. Not reported.

Notice And Motion For Terminating Sanctions Or In The Alternative, Issue Sanctions; And Request For Monetary Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-12-2009 Continued To Feb-11-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 610
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance, tentative ruling is adopted. Defendant Walgreen Co.'s motion for terminating sanctions is continued to February 11, 2009 at 9:00 a.m. in Department 610. The Court finds good cause to continue the hearing since Plaintiff is currently incarcerated. Both plaintiff and her counsel have advised the Court of her likely release date in February 2009. The Court will notify plaintiff of the time and place of the continued hearing. Commissioner: Bruce Chan. Not reported.

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Oct-27-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
David Yen of the Cartwright Law Firm (433.0444) counsel for plaintiff and moving to be relieved

No other appearances on this matter. Mr. Yen presented the Court with a copy of the letter sent to plaintiff, which the Court will file this date. MATTHEW QUINLAN'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL FOR PLAINTIFF SHARON WASH was granted. Counsel to prepare a form of order. (302/HEK) Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Court Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR #7077

MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
Off Calendar Oct-20-2008 Continued To Oct-27-08 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES: MATTHEW J. QUINLAN, ESQ. OF THE CARTWRIGHT LAW FIRM, INC.'S MOTION TO BE RELIEVED AS COUNSEL (FOR PLAINTIFF SHARON WASH).
APPEARANCES: David G. Yen, Esq. of The Cartwright Law Firm, Inc. 433-0444 moving to be relieved as counsel for pltf; no other appearances.
RULING: Hearing held. The Court will grant the motion if counsel provides proof that plaintiff, Ms. Wash, has been informed of the mandatory settlement conference, trial date, results of motion to compel and any other matters pending. The motion is continued to 10/27/08. If proof provided, no appearance is necessary. (302/HEK) JUDGE HAROLD E. KAHN, REPORTER LAVENA WARD, CSR 7077

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Oct-01-2008 Set For Trial On Apr-06-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 206; Settlement Conference On Mar-18-09 At 02:30 P.M. In Dept 505

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Responses To Discovery, And Request For Monetary Sanctions; Declarations Of William T. Mulvihill, Dennis P. Fitzsimons And Kristin Jo Custer In Support
Off Calendar Sep-29-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Kristin Jo Custer, Esq., for defendant and moving party: Walgreen Co.

No other appearance, tentative ruling is adopted. Defendant Walgreen Company's motion to compel responses to form and special interrogatories and request for production of documents is granted. Plaintiff is ordered to serve complete verified responses without objections and produce documents within 30 days of notice of this order. Monetary sanctions in the amount of $365 shall be paid within 30 days of notice of this order. Order signed in open court. Commissioner: Bruce Chan. Not reported.
